delta.text: at` function in vm.rs sets `n = 1` in the empty-string trivial cases, but it should be `n = 2` (we consumed 2 input
s to produce 1 result). With `n = 1`, the formula `total -= n - 1` becomes a no-op, so the empty oper
and stays on the stack and gets concatenated in the next iteration — that's why `"" .. "x"` becomes `"xx"`.

content.text: Found it. The `concat` function in vm.rs sets `n = 1` in the empty-string trivial cases, but it should be `n = 2` (we consumed 2 inputs to produce 1 result). With `n = 1`, the formula `total -= n - 1` becomes a no-op, so the empty operand stays on the stack and gets concatenated in the next iteration — that's why `"" .. "x"` becomes `"xx"`.

delta.text: green (no errors, only pre-existing unused-import warnings). No instrumentation was
in `concat()`, the two empty-string short-circuit branches (`is_empty(&v_tm1)` and `is_empty(&v
_tm2)`) set `n = 1`, but the loop tail then computes `total -= n - 1` and `set_top(top - (
n - 1))`, both no-ops when `n == 1`. The empty operand stayed on the stack and got conc
atenated again next iteration, so `"" .. "x"` became `"xx"` and `"" .. 1` became `"

11"`.

**Fix:** Set `n = 2` in both empty-string branches (matching C's default `n = 2`), since we

consumed 2 inputs and produced 1 result.

**New state:** Not re-tested (per instructions). Build compiles.
The mega-loop will re-test; expected output is `123`.

content.text:
Build is green (no errors, only pre-existing unused-import warnings). No instrumentation was added.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:935,940` — in `concat()`, the two empty-string short-circuit branches (`is_empty(&v_tm1)` and `is_empty(&v_tm2)`) set `n = 1`, but the loop tail then computes `total -= n - 1` and `set_top(top - (n - 1))`, both no-ops when `n == 1`. The empty operand stayed on the stack and got concatenated again next iteration, so `"" .. "x"` became `"xx"` and `"" .. 1` became `"11"`.

**Fix:** Set `n = 2` in both empty-string branches (matching C's default `n = 2`), since we consumed 2 inputs and produced 1 result.

**New state:** Not re-tested (per instructions). Build compiles. The mega-loop will re-test; expected output is `123`.

result:
Build is green (no errors, only pre-existing unused-import warnings). No instrumentation was added.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:935,940` — in `concat()`, the two empty-string short-circuit branches (`is_empty(&v_tm1)` and `is_empty(&v_tm2)`) set `n = 1`, but the loop tail then computes `total -= n - 1` and `set_top(top - (n - 1))`, both no-ops when `n == 1`. The empty operand stayed on the stack and got concatenated again next iteration, so `"" .. "x"` became `"xx"` and `"" .. 1` became `"11"`.

**Fix:** Set `n = 2` in both empty-string branches (matching C's default `n = 2`), since we consumed 2 inputs and produced 1 result.

**New state:** Not re-tested (per instructions). Build compiles. The mega-loop will re-test; expected output is `123`.
